title: Adaptive Regime Aggregation (ARA) — Canonical Schema
description: ARA is the Stage 2 completion architecture of DALE. It inherits the completed ECOA observational condition and resolves the active non-assigned completion field through declared coordination structures, returning a uniquely determined balanced completion readable through the inherited DALE architecture. ARA is not an observational layer, scoring engine, recommendation engine, or free design process.

architectural_position.note: ARA is not a tool and not an observational layer. It is the Stage 2 completion architecture operating within the inherited ECOA frame.
architectural_position.sequence: Stage 1: ECOA (Observation) → Stage 2: ARA (Completion) → Stage 3: Runner (Runtime Realization)
architectural_position.canonical_principle: ARA does not replace the inherited ECOA architecture. It inherits and completes unresolved architectural conditions within the same DALE-grounded frame.
architectural_position.canonical_law: All valid Stage 2 execution, integration, tooling, runtime realization, and traceability structures must preserve inherited ECOA continuity.

what_ara_is_not: a best-solution generator detached from the inherited DALE structure
an optimization engine detached from architecture

architectural_invariants.description: These conditions remain mandatory throughout all ARA execution.

assignment_continuity.meaning: ECOA-fixed values remain preserved throughout Stage 2

completion_field_integrity.meaning: Full unresolved architectural region inherited from ECOA

shared_metric_continuity.meaning: M = [-1, 1] preserved across observation and completion

reconstruction_continuity.meaning: DALE-readable outputs remain recoverable from lower-level architecture

anchor_integrity.meaning: Declared anchor regime remains explicit, stable, recoverable, and traceable

coordination_determinism.meaning: Stage 2 result uniquely determined from inherited architecture and declared rules

traceability_continuity.meaning: Every Stage 2 state recoverable through inherited architectural path

runtime_recoverability.meaning: Outputs remain serializable and runtime-continuous for Runner

AssignmentState.description: ECOA-Fixed=assigned during Stage 1, remains invariant | Completion-Eligible=non-assigned, enters completion field | ARA-Resolved=completed during Stage 2 | Runtime-Realized=operationally instantiated downstream | Structurally-Inactive=not active under current observation

AnchorMode.description: zero-anchor=A(v)=0 across active completion field, preserving uniform metric equilibrium | family-anchor=anchor values inherited from operational family structures, preserving subsystem-level balancing behavior. No additional undeclared anchor modes are admissible.

VariableReference.description: Reference to a canonical DALE variable with assignment state.

assigned_value.description: Value in [-1, 1] for assigned/resolved states. Null for completion-eligible or inactive.

Stage1ObservationObject.description: Inherited Stage 1 continuity anchor. The formal input condition of Stage 2. Functions as the continuity carrier between observation and completion.

AssignmentStateObject.description: Preserves fixed vs resolved distinction throughout execution. ECOA-fixed values remain non-revisable. Non-assigned values form the active completion field.

CompletionFieldObject.description: The unresolved architectural region inherited from ECOA. V_comp = V_proj minus V_fixed. Inherited directly from assignment-state structure — not manually selected, facilitator-defined, tool-generated, or runtime-improvised.

completion_membership.description: All inherited non-assigned variable IDs. Must be complete — no silent exclusions.

family_membership.description: Family continuity for each completion-field member

fixed_dependencies.description: ECOA-fixed variable IDs that condition completion of this field

AnchorObject.description: Preserves the stabilization regime of Stage 2 coordination. Only zero-anchor and family-anchor modes are admissible. Anchors may not be improvised, inferred heuristically, or substituted by hidden implementation defaults.

oneOf.description: Null when family-anchor values are inherited per-variable

anchor_family.description: Family ID where applicable for family-anchor mode. Must be inherited, recoverable, and traceable.

CarryForwardObject.description: Active inherited structural constraints from ECOA that continue conditioning completion throughout Stage 2. Not optional notes, facilitator comments, or external metadata — these are active inherited architectural conditions.

CoordinationObject.description: The declared coordination structure applied to the active completion field. Operates through fixed architectural rules — not freeform optimization, facilitator negotiation, heuristic ranking, or discretionary balancing.

completion_inputs.description: Active unresolved variable IDs entering coordination

determinism_status.description: True if unique solvability is preserved

ReconstructionObject.description: Preserves DALE-readable continuity. Stage 2 outputs must remain recoverable through inherited reconstruction paths from lower-level architecture.

TraceObject.description: Preserves execution lineage across the entire DALE stack. Traceability is a structural architectural requirement — not a reporting convenience.

Stage2OutputObject.description: The canonical Stage 2 output triple (E_comp, E_read, P_S2). All three components are mandatory. The output is not an isolated score, recommendation summary, or narrative — it is a completed Stage 2 architectural state in DALE-readable form.

E_comp.description: Completed value field. Contains all values resolved through Stage 2 completion. Does not overwrite ECOA-assigned constants.

E_read.description: Readable Stage 2 configuration. Reconstructed operational state of the completed architecture. Preserves operational readability, subsystem continuity, family organization, and reconstruction dependence.

P_S2.description: Stage 2 design package. Complete DALE-facing completion package for runtime realization, downstream execution, operational deployment, serialization, and traceable continuation into Runner.

assignment_continuity.description: Preserves fixed/resolved distinction. ECOA-fixed values, ARA-resolved values, and completion lineage remain distinct and recoverable.

Stage1ToStage2TransferPackage.description: Canonical Stage 1 → Stage 2 transfer package. Not a flat export object — a continuity-bearing architectural object. Stage 2 input is architectural, not merely informational.

variable_architecture.description: Complete inherited V_proj with assignment states

RuntimeTransferObject.description: Architecture-preserving transport object for Stage 2 → Stage 3 continuity. Functions as architectural continuity carrier — not a flat execution payload.

transition_requirements.description: Transition into Stage 2 is admissible only if all of the following remain recoverable.

zero_anchor.description: A(v) = 0 across the active completion field. Balancing occurs relative to neutral structural equilibrium inside inherited metric space.

family_anchor.description: Anchor values inherited from operational family structures. Balancing occurs relative to inherited subsystem-family conditions.
family_anchor.requirement: Anchor values must remain inherited, recoverable, and traceable. They may not be improvised, inferred heuristically, or substituted by hidden implementation defaults.

assignment: Fixed and resolved states collapse together
metric: Outputs remapped into detached scoring systems
Resolved values lose comparability with observational states
reconstruction: Readable outputs detach from lower-level architecture
shortcuts: AI recommendations from Stage 1 summaries without Stage 2 execution
